text: The video presents the technique of surgical excision of the right atrium muscle mass using a totally endoscopic bug set without robotic assistance. A fifty-one-year-old female was admitted to the hospital because of chest pain just one day before. She had a normal medical history. Trastroxic echocardiography revealed a giant neoplasm in the right atrium. A mass measuring forty-eight and thirty-six mm had a stalk attached to the anterior septum. The complete tomography for metastatic workup was negative. Because the tumor was mobile, moved deeply into the right ventricle during diastole that led to high risk of pulmonary embolism, so she had underwent an urgent operation to

The patient is positioned supine with the right side of the chest elevated at thirty degrees. Extended defibrillator pads are visible on the chest. Three trochars are placed in the right chest: one in the fifth intercostal space at the mid-axillary line for the 3D camera, one between the anterior axillary line and mid-clavicular line for the main surgical instrument, and one in the fourth anterior line for the second instrument. A two-centimeter transverse incision is made in the right groin for vasocannulation. The pericardium is opened two centimeters anterior to the frame knob, and a pericardium junction suture is placed.

The surgeon snares the superior vena cava using a pallium suture thread, while the inferior vena cava remains free. A rhiotomy is performed, and junction sutures are applied to expose the tumor. The mass appears gelatinous, reddish-brown, and sphere-shaped, measuring approximately five centimeters, and is attached to the fossa ovalis. It is completely detached from the septum and placed into a nylon bag in the thoracic cavity. The tumor is meticulously resected from its interatrial septum attachment and delivered into the nylon bag.

The surgeon inspects the head chambers using a three-dimensional angloscope to confirm complete tumor removal, ensuring no residual tissue remains. The right atrium is closed with a double-layer technique using a 4-0 polypropylene running suture.

The surgeon closes the pericardium with a running suture and places a 13-French drain in the pericardial cavity. The tumor-containing nylon bag is extracted through the 12-mm trocar hole after reducing the tumor volume. A 32-French drain is inserted through the 10-mm trocar position.

The hospital stayed with six days. The pathologic examination confirmed misdemeanor. The author hoped the video would be useful to you. Don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions about this video. Thank you.
